learningObjectives.title: Understand proof of delivery
learningObjectives.description: Explain POD as formal confirmation of successful shipment delivery.

learningObjectives.title: Understand POD data and capture methods
learningObjectives.description: Describe POD information fields and electronic/manual capture channels.

learningObjectives.title: Understand POD business impact
learningObjectives.description: Clarify how POD supports settlement, invoicing, and legal compliance.

sections.content: Proof of Delivery (POD) is the confirmation that goods have been successfully delivered to the customer or consignee. In SAP TM, POD is a critical transportation execution milestone that confirms completion of the delivery process and serves as the basis for freight settlement, invoicing and customer service.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Proof of Delivery confirms successful transportation completion and marks the transition from execution to settlement.

A driver delivers medical equipment to a hospital. The receiving officer verifies the shipment, signs electronically using the driver's mobile application and SAP TM immediately updates the Freight Order to Completed. The Proof of Delivery automatically triggers the freight settlement process.

sections.title: Delivery Completed vs Proof of Delivery
Best Practice
sections.content: Capture Proof of Delivery electronically whenever possible. Digital signatures and photographs improve accuracy and reduce delivery disputes.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A courier company implemented mobile Proof of Delivery with GPS location and customer signatures. Delivery disputes reduced by 70%, while freight settlement processing became significantly faster.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Proof of Delivery is not merely a status update. It is an official confirmation that supports freight settlement, customer billing and legal compliance.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Do not assume vehicle arrival automatically means delivery completion. Proof of Delivery requires confirmation from the receiving party.

items: Proof of Delivery confirms successful shipment completion.
POD is the basis for freight settlement.
Digital signatures and mobile applications are common POD methods.
POD reduces delivery disputes and improves traceability.
Transportation execution is formally completed after successful POD.
